title: podman network ls format doesn't work
body:
`podman network ls --format` doesn't work:

```
$ podman network ls  --format {{.ID}}
Error: template: listNetworks:1:14: executing "listNetworks" at <.ID>: can't evaluate field ID in type network.ListPrintReports
```

version:
```
podman.x86_64                                                         2:2.1.0-2.fc32                                                          @updates-testing
```

comments.body:
Where do you got the `{{.ID}}` from? I must say, i am only using version 2.0.4. 

`podman network ls --format {{.Name}}`
or
`podman network inspect podman --format {{.name}}`
works for me.

```
./bin/podman network ls --format json
```
there is no filed named `ID`

I believe this is a difference between us and Docker for now. We have plans
for a partial rewrite of the 'podman network's commands to address this but
it could take a while before we get to it.

On Thu, Oct 1, 2020, 08:09 zhangguanzhang <notifications@github.com> wrote:

> ./bin/podman network ls --format json
>
> there is no filed named ID
>
> —
> You are receiving this because you are subscribed to this thread.
> Reply to this email directly, view it on GitHub
> <https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/7865#issuecomment-702090491>,
> or unsubscribe
> <https://github.com/notifications/unsubscribe-auth/AB3AOCH2NUGNF5OQU3T6SOTSIRWQVANCNFSM4SAIOTVA>
> .
>

I had tried, `--format {{json .}}`. I'm not sure if that is supposed to work.

@mheon should I close this issue? or do you want to keep it open?
Hm. I'll tag it as a feature request and it can stay open.
Hopefully someone from the community can take this on.

I can take a look to this issue.
My first research leads me to `type NetworkConfigList` in `libcni`.

I will finish to setup my development environment for `podman` and I will investigate more.
You got it.  Ping us if you need any help.

Maybe I underestimate the problem.
At first sight I just though the ID was not printed, so adding `ID string` to `type NetworkConfigList` should have solve the problem.
It solves it partly, indeed there is no more error.
But the [ID is empty](https://framabin.org/p/?9863c4e329b75ac8#8aUOEE1mhdaxYM7fIDOjmgtqs53k4sUVWwkhBYWI/ug=) and I have the feeling that networks do not have ids in podman.
Even using the REST API through `curl` does not print the ID ([see](https://framabin.org/p/?1f5e399bc3e69914#qqNwbzQHLgdyK2pw2LWyCWhqhSZgd7GQQyTQ72kBABk=)).
Moreover, there is no ID into the [conflist file](https://framabin.org/p/?a0dc547234ab5eb9#+r8qMMtPZSeOtp9NdQdGDrSsJifLUhbyHCCVMte1Fnc=).

I can try to add ID to networks but the modifications will be deeper and take more time.
If you have advices so my modifications don't break anything and be as restrained as possible I will take them.

You are correct that we do not presently have IDs for networks and cannot easily add them. It's on the list for the networking rewrite I'd like to do eventually, but that won't be done for a while.

Submitting anything you have right now seems like a good idea, given that.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
Since this involves a rewrite of the network stack, it is not a good first issue.
